preview.summary: Group F favourites the Netherlands meet a dangerous Sweden side in a meeting of two European heavyweights with attacking firepower. The Dutch carry the deeper squad, but Sweden's strikers make this a potential group-defining contest.
preview.homeForm: The Netherlands are clear Group F favourites and one of the tournament's most settled European sides.
preview.awayForm: Sweden, now coached by Graham Potter, have historically started World Cups strongly and arrive among the group's tougher outsiders.
preview.homeKey: Virgil van Dijk, captain and defensive anchor at the back.
preview.awayKey: Viktor Gyökeres, prolific striker spearheading Sweden's attack.
storylines: Alexander Isak and Gyökeres give Sweden a fearsome strike pairing
Frenkie de Jong orchestrates the Dutch midfield
A rematch echoing their goalless 1974 World Cup group draw
preview.h2h: Across 20 prior meetings the Netherlands have the edge, last winning 2-0 in 2017 qualifying.
